title: {name} kommt in den Kindergarten
moral: New beginnings are scary, but brave children find friends and joy

beats.beat: Title page — {name} with backpack, looking excited/nervous
Morning routine — getting dressed, breakfast with {parent}
Walking/driving to Kindergarten in {city}
Arriving at Kindergarten — big door, other children
Saying goodbye to {parent} — a hug, {companion} for comfort
Exploring the room — cubbies, toys, art supplies
Meeting the teacher — friendly, welcoming
Circle time — everyone introduces themselves
Conflict/challenge — feeling shy, missing {parent}
{companion} provides comfort — {name} hugs it tight
Another child approaches — potential friend
Playing together — building blocks, painting
Snack time — sharing food, learning table manners
Outdoor play — playground, sandbox
Discovery — finding something interesting (bug, flower, puddle)
Helping someone — sharing a toy, comforting a crying child
Story time — teacher reads aloud, {name} listens with new friend
Singing/music — learning a new song together
Making something to take home — drawing for {parent}
Cleanup time — everyone helps tidy up the room
{parent} arrives for pickup — {name} runs to hug
Showing the drawing to {parent} — pride and joy
Walking home — talking about the day, excited for tomorrow
Bedtime — {name} tells {companion} about Kindergarten, falls asleep happy

vocabulary_constraints.toddler: max 8 words per sentence, 3-4 sentences per page
vocabulary_constraints.preschool: max 12 words per sentence, 4-5 sentences per page
vocabulary_constraints.early-reader: max 15 words per sentence, 5-7 sentences per page
